problem.summary: Compute C[b] = A[b] * B[b] for many independent row-major FP32 matrices with fixed small M, N, and K. The task is aimed at custom kernels that can compete with hipBLAS/rocBLAS, hipBLASLt, Composable Kernel, Triton, or framework-generated kernels when generic library setup, descriptor selection, or launch overhead is large relative to the tiny matrix work.
inputs: float* a: batch_count row-major matrices shaped [M, K]
float* b: batch_count row-major matrices shaped [K, N]
float* c: batch_count row-major output matrices shaped [M, N]
outputs: For each batch item b, c[b, row, col] equals sum_k a[b, row, k] * b[b, k, col]
constraints: No alpha, beta, transpose, bias, activation, or mixed-precision behavior in this seed task
Recommended scope is M, N, K <= 64; the optimized seed uses one CTA per output matrix and stages one full A and B matrix in shared memory
Batch count should be swept because batch=1 mostly measures launch overhead while large batches measure CTA-per-matrix throughput

verification.method: The harness computes a CPU FP32 reference for every output element and reports max_abs_error, max_rel_error, bad_count, and passed. Ported from the CUDA corpus; rerun on ROCm hardware before marking passed.

curation.notes: Ported from the CUDA corpus on 2026-05-20. CUDA-origin timing artifacts were removed; rerun on ROCm hardware before making performance claims.
